title: At what point does EoE segue into episode 25
display_title: At what point does EoE segue into episode 25
wikitext:
[]: At what point does EoE segue into episode 25?             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 3:49 am I'm a believer in the theory that the television and film endings are complementary,the same event viewed from two different perspectives,but I'd like to hear some opinions more educated than mine on precisely when one goes into another. If I remember correctly,last time I watched it I switched from film to episode twenty-five right right when Shinji starts screaming and jerking uselessly at the controls,watched all of twenty-five and most of twenty-six (up until the "school days" bit),switched back to EoE up until "Saraba da,Kaa-san" then finished episode twenty-six and then watched the very last bit. Probably not the exact sequence of events,but not bad,I think. Especially given how drunk I was last time I re-watched Eva. What can I say? I thought maybe Misato had the right idea. And you know what? She did. The only way to experience the months leading up to third impact are to experiene them drunk. >_>

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Fri Sep 18, 2009 4:36 am Either the Tang Moment when Kaworu appears and you see the waterdrop effect, or when Shinji gets strangly. Curiously, I don't recall Kaworu in 25/26. Never thought about that before..

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Sep 18, 2009 6:30 am I actually always thought 25 was the segue into EoE; Everything up until "Now Instrumentality begins" is a look insight Shinji's broken psyche after killing Kaworu, which could take place at any time before EoE or during the very beginning (probably before the alarms start going off). Then, when Instrumentality begins, we're already into the second half of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Apjak » Fri Sep 18, 2009 10:01 am IMO: EoTV = Psychological Ending EoE = Physical Ending Excepting that EoE is so good I am actually okay with the Mindraping that was EoTV, but I agree that it really shouldn't have a happy ending.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 18, 2009 11:43 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I actually always thought 25 was the segue into EoE; Everything up until "Now Instrumentality begins" is a look insight Shinji's broken psyche after killing Kaworu, which could take place at any time before EoE or during the very beginning (probably before the alarms start going off). Then, when Instrumentality begins, we're already into the second half of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 11:48 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I actually always thought 25 was the segue into EoE; Everything up until "Now Instrumentality begins" is a look insight Shinji's broken psyche after killing Kaworu, which could take place at any time before EoE or during the very beginning (probably before the alarms start going off). Then, when Instrumentality begins, we're already into the second half of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Sep 18, 2009 12:14 pm Namikiri wrote: How could this happen if Instrumentality wasn't underway?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Fri Sep 18, 2009 12:16 pm Merridian wrote: Pre-impact Instrumentality, kinda like the playground scene or the ‘imma choka bitch’ scene. Although this takes place AFTER Shinji’s in the air, I think there’s a little more leeway given—3I/GNR bends reality in strange ways, so I figured the event OF instrumentality wasn’t a clearly defined point in time; it’s just this haze that sorta-begins and sorta-ends without clear distinctions of ACTUALLY beginning or ACTUALLY ending. Therefore, it was already underway (in a weird sorta-paradoxical way) by the time the JSSDF invasion began. It’s fanwank, but it’s the best explanation I ever had.

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 1:41 pm Merridian wrote: Pre-impact Instrumentality, kinda like the playground scene or the ‘imma choka bitch’ scene. Although this takes place AFTER Shinji’s in the air, I think there’s a little more leeway given—3I/GNR bends reality in strange ways, so I figured the event OF instrumentality wasn’t a clearly defined point in time; it’s just this haze that sorta-begins and sorta-ends without clear distinctions of ACTUALLY beginning or ACTUALLY ending. Therefore, it was already underway (in a weird sorta-paradoxical way) by the time the JSSDF invasion began. It’s fanwank, but it’s the best explanation I ever had.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Sep 18, 2009 2:55 pm Namikiri wrote: I always thought that 3I=Instrumentality=3I,ie; the two are inseparable

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 3:13 pm Merridian wrote: The two are inseparable in the sense that 3I MUST occur in order for Instrumentality to come about, but seeing as how NGE brings in quantum physic anomalies like abstract quark behaviors,

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Sep 18, 2009 6:29 pm Namikiri wrote: I always thought that 3I=Instrumentality=3I,ie; the two are inseparable,rendering a thing like "Pre-Impact Instrumentality" impossible. However,it is definitely true that "GNR bends reality in strange ways," so it could make some odd kind of sense,and it would fit nicely with that theory of "Rei appears to Shinji in episode one because GNR can be everywhere in time all at once." I don't believe that theory at all,but that's outside the point. ^-^

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 6:41 pm thewayneiac wrote: This has been dubbed "Pre 3I Instrumentality", for lack of a better term. Is it perhaps just the terminology that bothers you? Or are you disputing the idea that the minds of Shinji, Asuka, Misato and Ritsuko are linked at this point?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Sep 18, 2009 7:27 pm To simplify what Merridian said about the separation/entanglement of 3I/Instrumentality: 3I Is the process in which a gigantic Anti AT-Field tangs the entire planet and releases all of the souls. If the Angels would have had their version of 3I then all Lilim life on Earth would've been wiped out with no redemption and Earth would've been reset for their usage (this is partially what happened during 2I which created the Dead Sea). Instrumentality is the process in which all souls become one collective mind inside Lilith. Both require intentionality; 3I requires a huge amount of S2 energy to generate a worldwide Anti AT Field which, apparently, takes a ton of life out of whomever is doing it (The MPEs, Lilith, Adam, etc.) which is why you need either an uber-powerful Seed of Life or 9 MPEs to execute it. Instrumentality requires Lilith absorbing all souls into her Doors of Guf after they've been released by 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Namikiri » Fri Sep 18, 2009 7:58 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: To simplify what Merridian said about the separation/entanglement of 3I/Instrumentality: 3I Is the process in which a gigantic Anti AT-Field tangs the entire planet and releases all of the souls. If the Angels would have had their version of 3I then all Lilim life on Earth would've been wiped out with no redemption and Earth would've been reset for their usage (this is partially what happened during 2I which created the Dead Sea). Instrumentality is the process in which all souls become one collective mind inside Lilith. Both require intentionality; 3I requires a huge amount of S2 energy to generate a worldwide Anti AT Field which, apparently, takes a ton of life out of whomever is doing it (The MPEs, Lilith, Adam, etc.) which is why you need either an uber-powerful Seed of Life or 9 MPEs to execute it. Instrumentality requires Lilith absorbing all souls into her Doors of Guf after they've been released by 3I.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 18, 2009 8:12 pm >_> <_< Ahem. If you mean "at what point does episode 25 segue into EoE", IMO it's this: EoE: People tanging/soul collections, Instrumentality breakup lines.... THEN EoTV: Shinji: What is this? Misato: The Human Instrumentality Project that your father was working on. Shinji: This is? Misato: It appears to be part of it. Asuka: Because the truth eludes even us. RITSUKO: Only what you can feel for yourself right now is fact. Rei: Within you. FUYUTSUKI: And your memory of those things will become the truth for you. RITSUKO: There are also some truths that change with time. Shinji: So, this is fact, the result of everything that was done. This... Asuka: There are a lot of realities. This is one of them. Misato: This is the result you wished for. Shinji: The one I wished for? Rei: Yes, annihilation, a world where no one is saved. Shinji: No! It's just that no one saved me! No one... RITSUKO: No one can save you. Kaji: This is what you wished for. Asuka: Destruction, death, the return to nothingness... It's what you wished for. Misato: This is reality. Shinji: What is reality? Rei: It's your world. HYUGA: It's your own world where you coexist with time, space, and other people. AOBA: It's a world where you yourself decide how to perceive and accept it. IBUKI: Right now, this is your world, where everything is given to you. TEXT: That is reality Misato: This is your world, which cannot be helped. Shinji: It's a world where everything's already set! RITSUKO (OFF): You're wrong. It's a world where you make the decisions. FUYUTSUKI (OFF): It's a world where your heart has decided that it is so. TEXT: This is reality Rei (OFF): Your will to live... Asuka (OFF): Your thoughts of wanting to die... They are all what you wished for. Shinji: You're saying even this darkness and this world of half measures are all things I wished for? Asuka (OFF): That's right. Kaji (OFF): You wished for a closed world, where you alone are comfortable. HYUGA (OFF): In order to protect the weakness of your heart. AOBA (OFF): In order to protect your pleasures in life. IBUKI (OFF): This is merely the result. Misato (OFF): For people cannot live in a world that is yours alone. They cannot live in a space that is closed off. Asuka (OFF): But you wished for the world, the world that surrounds you, to be closed off. RITSUKO (OFF): Your heart and mind eliminated everything you didn't like and wished for a world as solitary as possible. Rei (OFF): And that has led to this little haven for your soul. Asuka: This is one of the many ends that could occur. Misato: This is the end of the world that you have brought about. TEXT: And the journey towards instrumentality...will continue

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Sep 18, 2009 8:30 pm Namikiri wrote: why would this process necessarily cause ripples in time that move backward to affect what's already happened...?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Sep 18, 2009 9:06 pm Namikiri wrote: I mean,if you take daily "real" life as irreversible and inevitable,like the Eva series/movie,then shouldn't it also make sense that things now would ripple backwards? Why does my breakfast today not affect what I had for breakfast yesterday?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Sep 19, 2009 1:11 pm It's worth pointing out that evidence has been observed in real life (as in, our world, the ones talking about this bullshit), that things like quarks and other quantum particles can defy reality to some extent. Being in two places at once, jumping milliseconds forward and backward in time, existing out of thin air and vanishing again... These same sorts of terms are written in the room Rei was born in, and Rei's spirit has appeared to Shinji in the first episode, in his supernatural "Hell Train" dreams, in both Unit 00 and outside of it... It's entirely possible that Rei serves to show that Lilith's soul, or, possibly, ALL souls, can jump forward and backward through time and all such nonsense, since when Shinji becomes just a soul in episode 20, Ritsuko says he's "in a quantum state." Ergo, Instrumentality is an event transcending time, space, and reality because it's about souls, which are basically supernatural particles that can defy reality however the hell they want to.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Tue Sep 22, 2009 4:04 am Pre-impact Instrumentality, kinda like the playground scene or the ‘imma choka bitch’ scene. Although this takes place AFTER Shinji’s in the air, I think there’s a little more leeway given—3I/GNR bends reality in strange ways, so I figured the event OF instrumentality wasn’t a clearly defined point in time; it’s just this haze that sorta-begins and sorta-ends without clear distinctions of ACTUALLY beginning or ACTUALLY ending. Therefore, it was already underway (in a weird sorta-paradoxical way) by the time the JSSDF invasion began. It’s fanwank, but it’s the best explanation I ever had.

If I remember correctly,last time I watched it I switched from film to episode twenty-
